title: Disentangling magnetic and optical contributions in ultrafast dynamics of antiperovskite non-collinear antiferromagnets
authors: J. Kimak, Tomas Ostatnicky, M. Nerodilova, F. Johnson, O. Faiman, T. Trejtnar, D. Boldrin, F. Rendell-Bhatti, J. Zemen, B. Zou, A. P. Mihai, X. Sun, F. Yu, E. Schmoranzerova, L. Nadvornik, L. F. Cohen, P. Nemec
abstract: Non-collinear antiferromagnets are a class of spin-polarized antiferromagnets in which chiral spin textures give rise to Berry-curvature-driven phenomena, such as the anomalous Hall effect (AHE), without net magnetization. We investigate the properties of thin films of antiperovskite non-collinear antiferromagnetic metals Mn3NiN and Mn3GaN using pump-probe experiments. In both materials, we observe a strong dependence of pump-polarization-independent dynamics, induced by femtosecond laser pulses, on the angle between the sample normal and the direction of probe propagation. In Mn3NiN, where the presence of a sizable AHE indicates the Γ4g phase, the measured magnetooptical (MO) signals acquire an additional, strong dependence on the external magnetic field when the probe pulses are incident at nonzero angles. In contrast, in Mn3GaN, where the absence of AHE indicates the Γ5g phase, the measured signals do not depend on the magnetic field. Using probe-polarization-resolved measurements combined with full optical modeling based on Yeh's formalism, we quantitatively separate magnetic and non-magnetic contributions to the measured signals. We show that in Mn3NiN, the observed magnetic field dependence results from field-controlled redistribution of magnetic domain populations, enabled by their piezomagnetic moments and detected by a Kerr-like MO effect, while this effect is absent in Mn3GaN. Temperature-dependent measurements reveal a change from single-step to two-step quenching dynamics with increasing temperature in Mn3NiN. This behavior contrasts with the nearly temperature-independent quenching dynamics reported for the non-collinear antiferromagnetic Heusler compound Mn3Sn, but resembles the crossover from type-I to type-II demagnetization dynamics in metallic ferromagnets.
summary: This paper demonstrates a method to separate magnetic and optical signals in the ultrafast dynamics of non-collinear antiferromagnets. By studying Mn3NiN and Mn3GaN, the authors reveal how magnetic field and temperature influence domain redistribution and quenching processes. This provides deeper insight into the complex spin dynamics of antiperovskite materials.
main_problem: The challenge of disentangling magnetic (MOKE-like and Voigt) from non-magnetic (refractive index changes) contributions to the ultrafast dynamics in antiperovskite non-collinear antiferromagnets.
main_result: The researchers successfully separated magnetic quenching from optical heating and discovered that Mn3NiN exhibits a temperature-dependent transition from single-step to two-step quenching dynamics, unlike the temperature-invariant dynamics in Mn3Sn.
method: Time-resolved pump-probe spectroscopy combined with full optical modeling using Yeh's formalism and probe-polarization-resolved measurements.
model_or_system: Thin films of antiperovskite non-collinear antiferromagnetic metals Mn3NiN (Gamma4g phase) and Mn3GaN (Gamma5g phase). The model incorporates domain redistribution driven by piezomagnetic moments and uses a four-parameter fit for the permittivity tensor.
key_observables: Change in polarization rotation (delta beta), transient transmission (delta T/T), and magnetic-field-dependent (MFD) components.
important_parameters: Neele temperature (TN approx 246 K), quenching time (tauQ approx 0.4 ps), and sample tilt angle (Theta).
main_assumptions: Neglecting domain pinning and defects, and assuming equal initial domain populations in zero-field Mn3NiN.
figures_summary: Fig 1 shows the pump-probe setup and polarization angles; Fig 5 displays temperature-dependent rotation dynamics (single-step vs two-step); Fig 8 and 9 illustrate the domain variants and computed MOKE/Voigt signals for different tilt angles.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the problem of signal disentanglement, describes the experimental setup and materials, presents the optical modeling strategy, details the results for Mn3NiN and Mn3GaN (including field and tilt dependence), and concludes with a discussion on temperature-dependent quenching dynamics.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: FIG. 1. (a) Schematics of the experimental pump-probe setup used for inducing and detecting magnetic dynamics in samples of Mn3NiN or Mn3GaN. Orientations of linear polarization of pump and probe pulses are described by angles α and β, respectively. The pump-induced change of polarization rotation ∆β of the transmitted probe pulses is measured as a function of the time delay ∆t between the pump and probe pulses. Inset: Simplified top view of the setup. The samples can be tilted around the crystallographic a axis by an angle Θ between the magnetic field H and the plane of the samples. Direction of H is always perpendicular to the probe beam. (b), (c) The crystallographic and magnetic...

FIG. 3. Dynamics of rotation in Mn3NiN measured for various orientations of input probe polarization β for mag- netic fields (a) –530 mT and (b) +530 mT at temperature 25 K, sample tilt 20◦and probe wavelength 400 nm. (c) β- dependence extracted from (a) and (b) at a time delay 3 ps (symbols). Curves are fits by the model described in the text and Fig. 4.

FIG. 4. (a)-(d) 4 of the 8 variants of the Γ4g phase. The remaining domains are shown in Fig. 7. (e) Legend for (a), where directions of magnetic field +H for sample tilts 20° and 0° are depicted with a dark-yellow and a light-yellow arrow, respectively. Black arrow represents the piezo-induced magnetic moment MP, through which the magnetic field can prefer population of certain domains. (f)-(h) Computed β-dependence of probe polarization rotation reflecting (f) pump-induced magnetic quenching by 50 %, (g) pump-induced increase of the non-magnetic permittivity component ϵ(0) by 0.2 %, and (h) their sum for 4 domain configurations, as described in detail in Appendix B. Curves in (h) for ±H...

FIG. 5. Dynamics of magnetic-field-dependent part of the rotation signal in Mn3NiN at various temperatures (sym- bols). At temperatures 25 K (a) and 100 K (b), the signals show a single-step quenching, typical for type-I demagnetiza- tion, while at 200 K (c), the signal shows a two-step quench- ing, typical for type-II demagnetization. Curves: Fits by Eqs. (2) and (3) with A = 160 ± 20 µrad, B = 130 ± 20 µrad, τQ = 0.4 ± 0.2 ps, τ1 = 1.1 ± 0.5 ps, τ2 = 23 ± 5 ps for 25 K; A = 120 ± 15 µrad, B = 90 ± 15 µrad, τQ = 0.4 ± 0.2 ps, τ1 = 1.3±0.4 ps, τ2 = 25±5 ps for 100 K; and A1 = 12±2 µrad, A2 = 16±2 µrad, τQ,1 = 0.4±0.2 ps, τQ,2 = 5±1 ps for 200 K. (a) As-measured dynamics of rotation in Mn3NiN for 2 orthogonal polarization orientations of pump pulses measured at temperature 100 K with a magnetic field +530 mT, sample tilt 35◦, probe polarization β =135° and wavelength 532 nm.
